title: Thermodynamics and Kinetic Theory
subject: Physics
content:
# Thermodynamics and Kinetic Theory

## Quick Synopsis

Thermodynamics tracks heat, work, internal energy, and efficiency without needing microscopic details. Kinetic theory connects gas pressure and temperature to molecular motion.

## Must Know

- Internal energy of ideal gas depends only on temperature.
- Heat supplied can change internal energy and/or do work.
- Isothermal process has constant temperature; adiabatic process has no heat exchange.
- Second law limits conversion of heat into work.
- Kinetic theory explains pressure from molecular collisions.
- Degrees of freedom determine molar heat capacities.

## Formulas / Rules

- First law: ΔQ = ΔU + W by gas.
- Ideal gas: PV = nRT.
- Work in isobaric process: W = PΔV.
- Isothermal work: W = nRT ln(V2/V1).
- Adiabatic: PV^γ = constant; TV^(γ-1) = constant.
- Average kinetic energy per molecule: 3/2 kT.
- RMS speed: vrms = sqrt(3RT/M).

## JEE Focus

- Use PV diagrams: work is area under curve.
- For cyclic process, ΔU = 0 and net heat equals net work.
- Carnot efficiency depends only on reservoir temperatures in kelvin.
- Use Cp - Cv = R and γ = Cp/Cv for ideal gases.

## Common Traps

- Temperature must be in kelvin for thermodynamic formulas.
- Adiabatic does not mean isothermal.
- Heat is path function; internal energy is state function.
- Free expansion of ideal gas has zero work and no temperature change.

## 10-Minute Revision Checklist

- Can you identify process from PV graph?
- Can you compute work and heat in isothermal/adiabatic/isobaric paths?
- Can you use degrees of freedom for Cv and Cp?
- Can you calculate engine efficiency?
